message:
fix(fr-command): align stream NOGROUP/XGROUP errors + parse_stream_id with upstream

Three related stream-command parity fixes found via differential
probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701):

- parse_stream_id (br-frankenredis-s0v0): now accepts the bare
  "ms" form upstream's t_stream.c::streamGenericParseIDOrReply
  has supported since 5.0. "1" → (1, 0); "1-2" → (1, 2). Without
  this, XACK / XSETID / etc rejected integer-only IDs that
  upstream accepts. The "+" / "-" specials remain a separate
  helper since they're only valid in range-parsing contexts.

- NOGROUP wording (br-frankenredis-ud94): XPENDING / XCLAIM /
  XAUTOCLAIM / XREADGROUP now emit the upstream "No such key
  '<k>' or consumer group '<g>'" form (with XREADGROUP appending
  " in XREADGROUP with GROUP option") instead of the inverted
  "No such consumer group '<g>' for key name '<k>'" form. Matches
  legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c lines 2275/2945/3136/
  3388/3880. The XGROUP-internal "No such consumer group ... for
  key name" wording is preserved (used by SETID / DELCONSUMER /
  CREATECONSUMER once key existence is confirmed).

- XGROUP missing-key wording (br-frankenredis-qcmh): every XGROUP
  subcommand (CREATE / DESTROY / SETID / CREATECONSUMER /
  DELCONSUMER) now emits the upstream verbose "The XGROUP
  subcommand requires the key to exist. Note that for CREATE you
  may want to use the MKSTREAM option to create an empty stream
  automatically." message on a missing stream key, replacing the
  generic "ERR no such key". XINFO and bare XSETID remain on
  the generic wording since upstream uses the generic there too.

- redis.error_reply (br-frankenredis-xvlj follow-up): the helper
  now correctly applies upstream's first-word-as-code split for
  bodies that contain a space, regardless of whether the leading
  '-' was supplied. Re-verified against vendored redis: e.g.
  redis.error_reply('custom error') → 'custom error',
  redis.error_reply('NOAUTH foo bar') → 'NOAUTH foo bar', while
  redis.error_reply('err') still yields 'ERR err' (no space →
  default code).

Test updates:
  - 5 fr-command nogroup-/xgroup-error assertions realigned
  - core_stream.json: 6 fixture cases updated to upstream wording
  - eval_redis_error_reply_prepends_err_code_when_missing test
    extended with 2 more cases covering the first-word-as-code
    behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
